display.purpose: Hero h1 only, once per page, always on the Morning Sky gradient.

label.purpose: Uppercase text inside pill badges only.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: Default resting state for cards and the bottom CTA band.

shadows.name: lifted
shadows.purpose: Hover state for cards, paired with a small upward translate.

shadows.name: micro-ambient
shadows.purpose: Compact widgets inside the phone mockup.

shadows.name: cta-glow-ink
shadows.purpose: Hover glow on the Ink primary pill button.

shadows.name: cta-glow-light
shadows.purpose: Hover glow on the light secondary pill button.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default transition easing (--transition) used across hovers and state changes.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Consent-success overlay entrance and the scan-result pop-check animation.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Ink pill CTA — the site's highest-contrast call to action (header + hero download buttons).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 10px; background-color: #14151A; color: #ffffff; padding: 12px 26px; border-radius: 999px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px; white-space: nowrap; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background-color: #000000; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 10px 22px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.18); }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: White pill CTA used over the blue hero gradient where the Ink pill would lose contrast.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 10px; background-color: #ffffff; color: #1A1C1C; padding: 12px 26px; border-radius: 999px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 15px; white-space: nowrap; border: none; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 6px 18px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.16); transition: all 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background-color: #f2f4f8; box-shadow: 0 8px 20px rgba(15, 23, 42, 0.2); }

components.name: Badge Pill
components.description: Section tag — Cobalt Soft background, Cobalt uppercase label text, used once per section.
components.css: .ds-badge-pill { display: inline-block; background-color: #DDE1FF; color: #2A5CFF; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 999px; font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 800; font-size: 12px; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Feature Card
components.description: Paper card with hairline border, ambient shadow at rest, lifted shadow + upward translate on hover.
components.html:
Insightful Trends

Track Blood Pressure, Sugar, and Heart Rate on dashboard charts that show what's changed since your last reading.
components.css: .ds-card { background-color: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(26, 28, 28, 0.08); border-radius: 24px; padding: 32px; box-shadow: 0 18px 45px rgba(0,0,0,0.06); display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 14px; transition: all 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); max-width: 320px; } .ds-card:hover { transform: translateY(-5px); box-shadow: 0 28px 80px rgba(0,0,0,0.12); border-color: #DDE1FF; } .ds-card-icon { width: 50px; height: 50px; border-radius: 15px; background-color: #DDE1FF; color: #2A5CFF; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-card-title { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 19px; color: #1A1C1C; margin: 0; } .ds-card-body { color: #434656; font-size: 15px; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; }

components.name: Health Metric Widget
components.description: Signature component: a compact clinical-colored data card used in the interactive phone mockup, one per vital sign.
components.html:
120/80mmHg
Blood Pressure
components.css: .ds-widget { border-radius: 12px; border: 1px solid; padding: 12px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(0,0,0,0.02); transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1); width: 150px; } .ds-widget:hover { transform: scale(1.02); } .ds-widget-header { display: flex; justify-content: flex-end; } .ds-widget-icon { width: 24px; height: 24px; border-radius: 6px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-widget-val-row { display: flex; align-items: baseline; } .ds-widget-val { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 800; color: #1A1C1C; } .ds-widget-unit { font-size: 10px; color: #747688; margin-left: 2px; } .ds-widget-name { font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; color: #434656; }

components.name: Consent Slider
components.description: Signature component: the slide-to-grant-access control that dramatizes the time-bound, revocable consent principle.
components.html: Slide to Grant Access
components.css: .ds-consent-slider { position: relative; height: 48px; width: 260px; background-color: #EDF0F7; border-radius: 24px; border: 1px solid rgba(26,28,28,0.08); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; box-shadow: inset 0 2px 5px rgba(0,0,0,0.02); } .ds-consent-slider-text { font-size: 11px; font-weight: 700; color: #434656; } .ds-consent-slider-handle { position: absolute; left: 3px; width: 42px; height: 42px; border-radius: 50%; background-color: #2A5CFF; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(42,92,255,0.4); transition: transform 0.1s ease; }

narrative.overview: Sehat presents medical data the way a well-made clinical instrument presents a reading: exact, unhurried, and never showing off. The page makes one loud gesture — a deep morning-sky gradient hero band that frames the product like glass over an exhibit — and then goes quiet. Everything after that is white space, hairline borders, soft ambient shadows, and a single blue accent used with discipline. Precision is the persuasion; there is no stock photography, no stethoscope iconography, no teal-and-white "corporate healthcare" palette standing in for trust. Trust here is earned by showing the mechanism — a real scan, a real consent slider, a real revoke button — not by decorating around it. The system explicitly rejects the generic health-app playbook: no clip-art doctors, no gradient-text hero claims, no tiny uppercase eyebrows stacked above every section. Where the page needs a label, it uses a single pill badge per section, sparingly, in the one accent color the whole system is built around.
keyCharacteristics: One accent (cobalt #2A5CFF) carries all interactive and informational emphasis; everything else is ink, paper, or hairline gray.
Health-metric widgets are the one place color multiplies — each vital sign gets its own clinical hue, because distinguishing data at a glance is functional, not decorative.
Depth is ambient at rest, and only ever deepens as a direct response to hover — nothing floats unprovoked.
Pill shapes recur deliberately (buttons, badges, the consent slider) as the system's one consistent shape signature.

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Cobalt is the only color permitted to carry meaning or call for action outside the health-widget system. If a new element needs emphasis, reach for Cobalt before reaching for a new hue.

rules.name: The Clinical Palette Rule
rules.body: The four vital-sign colors exist only inside measurement widgets. They never leak into navigation, buttons, or marketing copy — the moment a "health color" appears outside a data context, it stops meaning data and starts meaning decoration.

rules.name: The Single Eyebrow Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracked labels appear only inside a pill badge shape, never as bare text above a heading. The pill shape alone isn't the full exception, though — repeating it on every section is still the banned cadence. It now bookends the page (first section and the closing security section only); the two sections between run on heading + copy alone.

rules.name: The Earned Depth Rule
rules.body: No element ships with Lifted shadow as its resting state. Elevation increases only in direct response to hover or an active interaction — a page that looks the same whether or not you're touching it.

dos: Do keep Cobalt (#2A5CFF) as the only cross-page accent color; every other hue on the page is either a neutral or scoped to a health-metric widget.
Do use the full pill shape (999px) for every button, badge, and slider — it's the system's one recurring shape signature.
Do let cards rest at Ambient shadow and only reach Lifted shadow on hover, paired with a small upward translate.
Do give each vital-sign widget its own clinical hue (Clinical Red, Pulse Teal, Amber Glucose, Deep Plum) so real data stays legible at a glance.
Do show the mechanism (a real scan animation, a real consent slider, a real revoke button) as the proof of the pitch — that is the trust-building device, not copy.
donts: Don't use stock-photo doctors, stethoscopes, or a teal-and-white "corporate healthcare" palette anywhere on the site.
Don't introduce gradient text, side-stripe borders, tiny uppercase eyebrows floating above headings (outside the pill-badge exception), or numbered 01/02/03 section scaffolding.
Don't let a health-widget color appear outside an actual measurement context.
Don't ship a card, button, or badge with a resting shadow heavier than Ambient.
Don't add a second accent color "for variety." If a section feels flat, reach for typography weight, spacing, or the existing Cobalt before introducing a new hue.
